column1: Here is an expanded comparative table including Shiva, Chenrezig, Dionysus, Pan, Krishna, Buddha, Mithras, Mitra, Maitreya, Amida Buddha, Jesus, Jizo Bosatsu, and related deities. The table lists their tradition, domains, mount (vahana), sacred abode, core iconography, and a common parable or mythic theme that resonates across traditions.

column7: Common Parable / Theme Shared Across Traditions

column3: Destruction, transformation, asceticism, fertility, yoga, arts
column6: Trident, drum, crescent moon, snake, blue throat, third eye
column7: Dance of destruction and rebirth (cycle of creation and dissolution)

column4: Not typically depicted with a mount
column6: Multiple arms, lotus, prayer beads, deer skin
column7: Vow to save all beings before attaining final liberation (self-sacrifice for others)

column3: Wine, festivity, ecstasy, madness, theater, rebirth
column4: Panther, leopard, chariot by big cats
column7: Death and rebirth through ecstasy and dismemberment (initiation, transformation)

column3: Nature, wildness, shepherds, rustic music, fertility

column7: Instinct and wildness as sources of both fear and joy (panic, liberation)
